wt - LLM Quick Context

Purpose
- wt is a Git worktree manager written in Go.
- It helps create, checkout, list, and remove worktrees with predictable paths.

Primary commands
- wt create <branch> [base-branch]
- wt checkout <branch>  (alias: wt co)
- wt list               (alias: wt ls)
- wt remove <branch>    (alias: wt rm)
- wt pr [number|url]    (GitHub PR branch checkout via gh)
- wt mr [number|url]    (GitLab MR branch checkout via glab)
- wt info               (show effective location strategy/pattern)
- wt init               (install shell integration)

Core workflow rule
- Do not switch branches in the user's main checkout for task work.
- Create an isolated worktree for each task with wt create.

Agent-safe workflow
1) Create task worktree: wt create feat/short-description
2) Work only inside that worktree path
3) Run checks: go test ./... && go build -o bin/wt . && go run e2e/run.go
4) Commit with Conventional Commits
5) Push branch and open PR/MR
6) Cleanup after merge: wt rm <branch>

Configuration
- Config file: ~/.config/wt/config.toml (or WT_CONFIG / --config)
- Key settings: root, strategy, pattern, separator
- Env overrides: WORKTREE_ROOT, WORKTREE_STRATEGY, WORKTREE_PATTERN, WORKTREE_SEPARATOR

Hooks
- Supported hooks: pre/post create, checkout, remove, pr, mr
- Hook env vars: WT_PATH, WT_BRANCH, WT_MAIN, WT_REPO_NAME, WT_REPO_HOST, WT_REPO_OWNER
- Disable hooks: WT_HOOKS_DISABLED=1

Dependencies
- Required: git
- Optional: gh (wt pr), glab (wt mr)
- Build: Go 1.24+

Canonical docs
- AGENTS.md: agent workflow and project-specific guardrails
- README.md: command usage, config, examples
- CONTRIBUTING.md: contribution and CI workflow

Notes for non-interactive environments
- Prefer explicit args over interactive prompts: wt co <branch>, wt rm <branch>, wt pr <id>, wt mr <id>
